title: Group 1 — Alkali Metals
elements: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr
mnemonic_en: Little Napoleon Konquered Rome, Boldly Conquering Frontiers
mnemonic_hi: Laila Na Kar Rabya Se Fight (Li Na K Rb Cs Fr)
explanation: First letters map to Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr in order down the group. All have 1 valence electron, are soft, highly reactive metals, and reactivity increases down the group.

title: Group 2 — Alkaline Earth Metals
elements: Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra
mnemonic_en: Be My Cat, Sir Barium Rat
mnemonic_hi: Be Mera Cutta, Sir Bada Rakshas (Be Mg Ca Sr Ba Ra)
explanation: 2 valence electrons each, form +2 ions. Reactivity and basic character of hydroxides increase down the group, while lattice energy decreases.

title: Group 13 — Boron Family
mnemonic_hi: Bina Aloo Gali Idhar Tha (B Al Ga In Tl)
explanation: Boron is a non-metal (electron deficient, forms covalent compounds); the rest are metals. Inert pair effect makes Tl(I) more stable than Tl(III).

title: Group 14 — Carbon Family
mnemonic_hi: Chuha Sona Ghar Sunta Pila (C Si Ge Sn Pb)
explanation: Carbon uniquely catenates (long C-C chains) and forms strong pi bonds. Inert pair effect makes Pb(II) more stable than Pb(IV), the reverse of carbon's typical +4 preference.

title: Group 15 — Nitrogen Family (Pnictogens)
mnemonic_hi: Nitin Paisa Aur Sabzi Bina (N P As Sb Bi)
explanation: N2 is highly stable (strong triple bond); metallic character and basicity of hydrides increase down the group while thermal stability of hydrides decreases.

title: Group 16 — Oxygen Family (Chalcogens)
mnemonic_hi: Oont Sasta, Se Tera Polo (O S Se Te Po)
explanation: Oxygen forms strong pi bonds and is a gas (O2/O3); sulphur exists in ring allotropes (S8). Metallic character increases down the group (Po is a metal).

title: Group 17 — Halogens
mnemonic_hi: Fauji Chala Bhaage, Idhar Aata (F Cl Br I At)
explanation: All exist as diatomic molecules, highly reactive non-metals forming -1 ions. Oxidising power and electronegativity decrease down the group (F is strongest oxidiser); acid strength of HX increases down the group (HI is the strongest acid).

title: Group 18 — Noble Gases
elements: He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe, Rn
mnemonic_en: Henry Never Argues, Kids Xerox Randomly
mnemonic_hi: Hemant Ne Argue Kiya, Xerox Rakh (He Ne Ar Kr Xe Rn)
explanation: Complete octets make these largely inert; only the heavier members (Kr, Xe, Rn) form compounds, and only with the strongest electronegative elements F and O (e.g. XeF2, XeF4, XeF6, XeOF4).

title: Period 2 elements
elements: Li, Be, B, C, N, O, F, Ne
mnemonic_en: Lazy Beavers Build Cozy Nests On Frozen Nights
mnemonic_hi: Lil Beta Bola, Chai Nahi, Oats Fine Nahi (Li Be B C N O F Ne)
explanation: Period 2 shows the sharpest left-to-right periodic trends (small atoms, big jumps in ionisation energy and electronegativity) of any period — a good period to memorise trend direction on.

title: Period 3 elements
elements: Na, Mg, Al, Si, P, S, Cl, Ar
mnemonic_en: Naughty Magpies Always Sing Perfect Silly Choruses, Argue!
mnemonic_hi: Namak Mangta Aloo, Simple Papa Sun Chala, Aaram (Na Mg Al Si P S Cl Ar)
explanation: Period 3 is the most commonly tested period for atomic radius/ionisation energy trend questions — practice ranking these 8 elements by size and IE1 using effective nuclear charge.

title: Most electronegative elements (ranked)
mnemonic_en: FONCl — 'Fon-Cul' ranks the top 4 electronegative elements
mnemonic_hi: F O N Cl — याद रखें 'फॉनकल'
explanation: After fluorine (most electronegative element, EN 3.98), oxygen and nitrogen/chlorine follow closely — useful for quickly predicting bond polarity and which atom pulls electron density in a bond.

title: Reactivity (Activity) Series of Metals
elements: K, Na, Ca, Mg, Al, Zn, Fe, Pb, H, Cu, Ag, Au
mnemonic_en: Please Stop Calling Me A Zebra, Instead Provide Him Cool Silver Gold (K Na Ca Mg Al Zn Fe Pb H Cu Ag Au)
mnemonic_hi: Kabhi Na Cry, Mg Aur Zn Foren Padhe, Hum Cu Ag Sona (K Na Ca Mg Al Zn Fe Pb H Cu Ag Au)
explanation: Metals higher in this series are more reactive, displace metals below them from solution, and react more readily with acids; H is included as a reference point — metals above H liberate H2 gas from dilute acids, metals below do not.

title: Lanthanoid Contraction
mnemonic_en: 'The shrinking family photo' — each lanthanoid is a little smaller than the last
mnemonic_hi: हर लैंथेनॉइड पिछले से थोड़ा छोटा — 4f electrons ki poor shielding ki wajah se
explanation: Poor shielding by 4f electrons causes a steady size decrease across the lanthanoid series; this is why Zr (period 5) and Hf (period 6) end up almost the same size — a favourite JEE conceptual question.

title: Diagonal Relationship
mnemonic_en: 'Diagonal Neighbours Act Alike' — Li behaves like Mg, Be behaves like Al, B behaves like Si
mnemonic_hi: Li-Mg, Be-Al, B-Si — pados wale jaise dikhte hai (similar charge/size ratio)
explanation: The first element of a group often resembles the element diagonally below-right in the next group, because both have a similar charge-to-size ratio (polarising power) despite being in different groups/periods.
